**Sergio Darío Herrera Month** (born 15 March 1981 in [Barrancabermeja](/wiki/Barrancabermeja "Barrancabermeja")) is a [Colombian](/wiki/Colombian_people "Colombian people") [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") manager and former player who played as a [forward](/wiki/Forward_(association_football) "Forward (association football)"). He is the current assistant manager of Venezuelan club [Deportivo Táchira](/wiki/Deportivo_T%C3%A1chira_F.C. "Deportivo Táchira F.C.").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sergio_Herrera_(footballer,_born_1981)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Herrera began his career in 1999 with [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera "Alianza Petrolera"), but stayed for just one season before moving to play in the [Argentine Second Division](/wiki/Argentine_Second_Division "Argentine Second Division") with [Club Almagro](/wiki/Club_Almagro "Club Almagro"). However, he was soon on the move again, going back to [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") and signing for [América de Cali](/wiki/Am%C3%A9rica_de_Cali "América de Cali") for the start of the 2000 [Mustang Cup](/wiki/Mustang_Cup "Mustang Cup"), which he won with América. He stayed in Cali for three seasons before moving back to the club where he had started, [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera "Alianza Petrolera"), where he again stayed for only one season before he switched back to [América de Cali](/wiki/Am%C3%A9rica_de_Cali "América de Cali") where he had a successful season, scoring 13 goals in 22 matches.

This sparked the interest of [Saudi Premier League](/wiki/Saudi_Premier_League "Saudi Premier League") side [Al Ittihad](/wiki/Al-Ittihad_(Jeddah) "Al-Ittihad (Jeddah)"). However, he struggled to make an impact in [Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia") and transferred to Brazil to play with [Atlético Paranaense](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Paranaense "Atlético Paranaense").

On 5 February 2010, [Columbus Crew](/wiki/Columbus_Crew "Columbus Crew") of [MLS](/wiki/Major_League_Soccer "Major League Soccer") signed the Colombian forward from [Deportivo Cali](/wiki/Deportivo_Cali "Deportivo Cali") on an undisclosed terms.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

He was named to the 18-man roster for the Crew's [CONCACAF Champions League](/wiki/CONCACAF_Champions_League "CONCACAF Champions League") quarterfinal first leg against [Deportivo Toluca](/wiki/Deportivo_Toluca "Deportivo Toluca") of Mexico on 9 March 2010 in [Columbus, Ohio](/wiki/Columbus,_Ohio "Columbus, Ohio"), but did not enter the match.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Herrera was again an unused substitute for the Black & Gold in the return leg at [Deportivo Toluca](/wiki/Deportivo_Toluca "Deportivo Toluca") on 17 March 2010 in [Toluca, Mexico](/wiki/Toluca,_Mexico "Toluca, Mexico").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

He was released on 29 June 2010 having played just one league minute for the Crew.[[5]](#cite_note-5) This broke the previous club record for fewest career minutes held by [Ricci Greenwood](/wiki/Ricci_Greenwood "Ricci Greenwood") with 7, set 14 years earlier during the team's inaugural season. After his release from the Crew, Herrera signed for Venezuelan club [Deportivo Táchira](/wiki/Deportivo_T%C3%A1chira "Deportivo Táchira").
